name: Classic Challa
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the dry yeast in warm water in a large bowl.
2. When dissolved, add sugar, salt, and half of the flour.
3. Mix well. Add eggs and oil, then slowly stir in most of the remaining flour.
4. The dough will be quite heavy; add a little more flour to keep it manageable, knead until smooth and elastic.
5. Place the dough in an oiled bowl, turning so the top is coated as well. Cover with a damp towel and let rise in a warm place for about 2 hours, punching down every 20 minutes.
6. Divide the dough into three parts. Shape into loaves (or twist) and place in greased loaf pans or baking sheets.
7. Let rise until doubled in bulk. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
8. Brush the tops of the loaves with beaten egg and sprinkle with seeds.
9. Bake for 30 to 45 minutes, or until brown. Remove from oven and cool on a rack.
